2603.07444.title: HLER: Human-in-the-Loop Economic Research via Multi-Agent Pipelines for Empirical Discovery
2603.07444.paper_summary: Large language models (LLMs) have enabled agent-based systems that aim to automate scientific research workflows. Most existing approaches focus on fully autonomous discovery, where AI systems generate research ideas, conduct analyses, and produce manuscripts with minimal human involvement. However, empirical research in economics and the social sciences poses additional constraints: research questions must be grounded in available datasets, identification strategies require careful design, and human judgment remains essential for evaluating economic significance. We introduce HLER (Human-in-the-Loop Economic Research), a multi-agent architecture that supports empirical research automation while preserving critical human oversight. The system orchestrates specialized agents for data auditing, data profiling, hypothesis generation, econometric analysis, manuscript drafting, and automated review. A key design principle is dataset-aware hypothesis generation, where candidate research questions are constrained by dataset structure, variable availability, and distributional diagnostics, reducing infeasible or hallucinated hypotheses. HLER further implements a two-loop architecture: a question quality loop that screens and selects feasible hypotheses, and a research revision loop where automated review triggers re-analysis and manuscript revision. Human decision gates are embedded at key stages, allowing researchers to guide the automated pipeline. Experiments on three empirical datasets show that dataset-aware hypothesis generation produces feasible research questions in 87% of cases (versus 41% under unconstrained generation), while complete empirical manuscripts can be produced at an average API cost of $0.8-$1.5 per run. These results suggest that Human-AI collaborative pipelines may provide a practical path toward scalable empirical research.
2603.07444.paper_summary_zh: 大型語言模型（LLMs）使得基於代理的系統得以實現，旨在自動化科學研究工作流程。大多數現有的方法專注於完全自主的發現，AI 系統在最小的人類參與下生成研究想法、進行分析並撰寫手稿。然而，經濟學和社會科學的實證研究面臨額外的限制：研究問題必須基於可用數據集，識別策略需要精心設計，人類判斷對於評估經濟意義仍然至關重要。我們介紹 HLER（人類參與的經濟研究），這是一種多代理架構，支持實證研究自動化，同時保留關鍵的人類監督。該系統協調專門的代理進行數據審核、數據剖析、假設生成、計量經濟分析、手稿撰寫和自動審查。一個關鍵的設計原則是數據集感知的假設生成，其中候選研究問題受到數據集結構、變量可用性和分佈診斷的限制，從而減少不可行或幻覺的假設。HLER 進一步實施了一種雙循環架構：一個問題質量循環，用於篩選和選擇可行的假設，以及一個研究修訂循環，在該循環中，自動審查觸發重新分析和手稿修訂。人類決策閘在關鍵階段嵌入，允許研究人員指導自動化流程。對三個實證數據集的實驗表明，數據集感知的假設生成在 87% 的案例中產生可行的研究問題（相比之下，無限制生成僅為 41%），而完整的實證手稿的平均 API 成本為每次運行 $0.8-$1.5。這些結果表明，人類與 AI 的協作流程可能為可擴展的實證研究提供了一條實際的途徑。
2603.07444.author: Chen Zhu et.al.
